--- SOURCE 4: Functions | Research Institute for Tropical Medicine ---
URL: https://ritm.gov.ph/transparency/functions/

SUMMARY:
Undertake research activities in the diagnosis, control and prevention of tropical diseases that are major causes of mortality and morbidity in the Philippines.

FULL CONTENT:
# 

![](https://ritm.gov.ph/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/MASTHEAD-FINALv2.png)

# Functions

![FUNCTIONS](http://intuitivsolutions.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/04/FUNCTIONS.jpg)

![FUNCTIONS](http://intuitivsolutions.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/04/FUNCTIONS.jpg)

The primary function of RITM has been two-fold: to undertake research in the prevention, diagnosis and treatment of tropical diseases of public health importance and to produce vaccines for the control of vaccine-preventable diseases. To be relevant to the national health policy and strategy, the research efforts have been directed towards the development of new diagnostic techniques as well as effective and efficient strategies for the control of infectious and/or tropical diseases. Its research efforts are being disseminated through local and international publications, presented in various scientific meetings and submitted to the health policy-making bodies.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Ongoing research is vital for developing novel treatments, diagnostic tools, and preventive strategies, enhancing health outcomes in regions plagued by tropical diseases.
Innovative solutions are essential to tackle complex health challenges, enabling effective responses to emerging tropical diseases and improving public health in affected areas.
Addressing health equity ensures that all populations, regardless of socioeconomic status, have access to advances in tropical medicine, ultimately improving overall global health.
Collaboration among healthcare providers, researchers, and policymakers fosters a comprehensive approach to tackling tropical diseases and enhances the effectiveness of health interventions.
A sustained commitment to global health improvement is crucial for reducing the burden of tropical diseases, ensuring healthier futures for vulnerable populations worldwide.
